page: Amazon Web Services r8in.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at a dedicated clock speed of 3.9 GHz, this server allocates 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The hardware configuration delivers an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio, which is designed for memory-intensive workloads.

The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring low virtualization overhead and dedicated resource allocation. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps and is optimized for Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) connectivity. However, the r8in.2xlarge does not include any local NVMe storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density per vCPU allows organizations to run memory-bound applications without paying for unneeded compute cores. This resource balance makes the instance suitable for workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity but moderate CPU resources.
description: Amazon Web Services r8in.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance designed for workloads requiring high memory-to-vCPU ratios. Powered by a dedicated Intel processor running at 3.9 GHz, the server provides 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores and 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps and is optimized for EBS storage. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, representing a tradeoff that prioritizes network-attached storage and memory capacity over local disk speed. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications by preventing the over-provisioning of compute resources. It is suited for relational databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 instance featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel processing for memory-intensive workloads.

Dedicated Intel x86_64 processor running at 3.9 GHz
AWS Nitro hypervisor with 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
